---
title: "Mandi Urbizo-Haukjaer, Ph.D."
format: html
---

:::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; flex-direction: row-reverse;"}
![](images/37660E7C-4F6F-4C07-9769-F0553B182BE7.jpeg){alt="Mandi's Photo" style="margin-left: 40px;" fig-alt="Mandi's Photo" width="800px"}



<div>
Mandi Urbizo-Haukjaer is a quantitative researcher and analyst with expertise in advanced statistical methods, program evaluation, survey research, and applied social science. She earned her Ph.D. in Community Psychology with a minor in Quantitative Statistics from the University of Illinois Chicago. She also holds an M.A. in Applied Psychology from the University of Cincinnati with graduate certificates in Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies and Applied Behavior Analysis.

</div>
::::
:::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; flex-direction: row-reverse;"}
<div>

Mandi’s work centers on using data to understand complex social issues, evaluate programs and policies, and inform decision-making. Her research has examined topics including gender-based violence, higher education, student success, financial wellness, and the effectiveness of public policies and systems. She has extensive experience working with large-scale quantitative and qualitative datasets, employing methods such as mixed-effects modeling, interrupted time series analysis, structural equation modeling, survey analytics, and program evaluation.

</div>
::::
<div>

Beyond academia, Mandi has partnered with nonprofits and community organizations to conduct assessments, evaluate initiatives, and translate research findings into actionable recommendations. She is passionate about making data accessible to diverse audiences and helping organizations leverage evidence to improve outcomes, advance equity, and drive meaningful change.

<div>

Mandi is currently exploring opportunities in research, analytics, program evaluation, and data-driven strategy across higher education, nonprofit, public, and private sector organizations.

<div>

![](images/MUH%20CV.pdf){fig-align="center" width="9in" height="7in"}
